mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in industrial production over the next 60 trading days signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decline in the unemployment rate over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE < 4% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10% increase in housing starts over the next 20 trading days signals a strengthening supply environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real GDP over the next 60 trading days signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate over the next 5 trading days signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 0.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the unemployment rate over the next 20 trading days signals a weakening labor market that boosts wage growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5% increase in real GDP and a 10% increase in industrial production over the next 60 trading days signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 5% and INDPRO > 10% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
